meta.title: SF-SPADE-5: Dynamic Pipelines and Snowpark Delivery

questions.stem: A team wants to ingest newly arrived files from cloud storage into Snowflake with event-driven automation. Which feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpipe auto-ingest is designed for continuous file ingestion triggered by cloud event notifications.

**B** is incorrect because Time Travel is for historical data access.

**C** is incorrect because zero-copy cloning copies metadata for objects.

**D** is incorrect because search optimization improves selective queries, not ingestion.

[Snowpipe auto-ingest](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-auto)

questions.stem: What does a Snowflake stream primarily track?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because a stream records change data capture information representing changes in a source object between offsets.

**B** is incorrect because credit usage is handled by billing and monitoring views.

**C** is incorrect because role changes are not what streams track.

**D** is incorrect because network captures are unrelated.

[Streams introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro)

questions.stem: A task should run only after another task succeeds instead of using a fixed schedule. What Snowflake capability enables this?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowflake tasks can be linked into task graphs so child tasks run after predecessor completion.

**B** is incorrect because replication copies data across regions or accounts.

**C** is incorrect because Fail-safe is for disaster recovery windows.

**D** is incorrect because search optimization is query acceleration.

[Task graphs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-graphs)

questions.stem: A team wants a declarative pipeline object that refreshes query results to meet a target freshness SLA. Which Snowflake object should they choose?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because dynamic tables refresh query results based on dependencies and target lag requirements.

**B** is incorrect because stages store files.

**C** is incorrect because sequences generate numeric values.

**D** is incorrect because tags store governance metadata.

[Dynamic tables introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables-intro)

questions.stem: Which Snowflake development framework provides DataFrame-style APIs in languages such as Python for in-platform data processing?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpark is Snowflake's developer framework for DataFrame-style programming and other in-platform development patterns.

**B** is incorrect because Snowsight is the UI.

**C** is incorrect because Time Travel is a data retention feature.

**D** is incorrect because failover groups manage replication and failover.

[Snowpark developer guide](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index)

questions.stem: Which SQL command is most appropriate for implementing an upsert pattern in Snowflake?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `MERGE` combines conditional insert, update, and delete logic for source-to-target synchronization.

**B** is incorrect because `UNDROP` restores dropped objects.

**C** is incorrect because `GRANT` manages privileges.

**D** is incorrect because warehouse suspension controls compute state.

[MERGE command](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/merge)

questions.stem: A query becomes faster after adding a selective filter on a well-clustered column. Which Snowflake behavior is helping most?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowflake uses metadata on micro-partitions to prune irrelevant data when predicates align well with stored values.

**B** is incorrect because cache invalidation would not explain faster selective scans.

**C** is incorrect because Fail-safe is unrelated to query scan performance.

**D** is incorrect because task graphs are orchestration, not scan optimization.

[Micro-partitions and data clustering](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-micropartitions)

questions.stem: Which are common ingestion design choices in Snowflake? (Select TWO)

options.text: Using Snowpipe for continuous file ingestion
Relying on IAM instance profiles inside a warehouse
Using VPC route tables as stages

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowflake ingestion commonly uses stages for files and Snowpipe for continuous event-driven loading.

**C** is incorrect because warehouse internals do not use IAM instance profiles that way.

**D** is incorrect because route tables are networking objects, not file stages.

[Data loading overview](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-overview)

questions.stem: Which statements about tasks are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Tasks can run on a schedule
Tasks are the only way to query a table

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because tasks support scheduled execution and predecessor-based orchestration graphs.

**C** is incorrect because tables can be queried directly without tasks.

**D** is incorrect because tasks still rely on compute resources when they run.

[Tasks introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro)

questions.stem: What does `TARGET_LAG` on a dynamic table express?

options.text: The desired freshness lag relative to base data
The number of streams attached to the table

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `TARGET_LAG` defines the freshness target for a dynamic table relative to its upstream data, not a fixed refresh interval.

**B** is incorrect because warehouse auto-suspend is a compute setting configured on the warehouse, not on the dynamic table freshness policy.

**C** is incorrect because Fail-safe retention is a separate data protection feature.

**D** is incorrect because `TARGET_LAG` does not track the number of streams associated with a table.

[Dynamic tables target lag](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables/target-lag)

questions.stem: Which Snowflake feature lets developers write Python-based transformations that execute close to the data?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpark for Python enables DataFrame-style code and other Python logic that runs with Snowflake processing.

**B** is incorrect because search optimization is for query acceleration.

**C** is incorrect because resource monitors track credit usage.

**D** is incorrect because replication groups support continuity.

[Snowpark for Python](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/python/index)

questions.stem: Which Snowflake SQL features are commonly used for advanced transformation work? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** and **B** are correct because `QUALIFY` is commonly used to filter the results of window functions and `FLATTEN` is used to expand semi-structured arrays or objects into rows.

**C** is incorrect because `COPY INTO` is primarily a data loading command, not an advanced transformation feature.

**D** is incorrect because `ALTER WAREHOUSE` manages compute settings rather than transforming query results.

[FLATTEN table function](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/flatten)

questions.stem: What happens to a stream offset when its captured changes are consumed by a DML statement that reads the stream?

options.text: The offset advances to reflect the consumed changes
All historical table versions are deleted

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because when a stream is consumed by a transaction, its offset advances so later reads see subsequent changes.

**B** is incorrect because streams are not dropped by ordinary consumption.

**C** is incorrect because table history is not deleted.

**D** is incorrect because warehouse state is separate.

[Streams introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro)

questions.stem: When would a team often prefer a dynamic table over manually orchestrated transformation SQL with tasks?

options.text: When they want declarative refresh based on freshness requirements
When they need object storage lifecycle rules
When they want to manage VPC firewall policies
When they need to replace all warehouse sizing decisions

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because dynamic tables provide a declarative way to maintain transformed results according to target freshness.

**B** is incorrect because storage lifecycle rules are unrelated.

**C** is incorrect because firewall policies are outside Snowflake data pipelines.

**D** is incorrect because warehouse sizing still matters.

[Dynamic tables introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables-intro)

questions.stem: A team must ingest data files from an external location before transformation. Which Snowflake object commonly defines that landing location?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because a **stage** defines a named location for loading data files from internal or external storage before downstream processing.

**B** is incorrect because a tag stores governance metadata, not file locations.

**C** is incorrect because a failover group supports replication and disaster recovery.

**D** is incorrect because a sequence generates numeric values.

[Stages overview](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-overview)

questions.stem: Which capabilities are associated with Snowpark? (Select TWO)

options.text: Stored procedure development in supported languages

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpark supports DataFrame programming and language-based stored procedures while still coexisting with SQL and compute choices.

**C** is incorrect because SQL remains a core Snowflake capability.

**D** is incorrect because compute is still required.

[Snowpark developer guide](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index)

questions.stem: Which Snowflake service is specifically intended to improve performance for highly selective queries?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because search optimization is the feature built to improve certain selective lookup and join patterns.

**B** is incorrect because Fail-safe is for recovery.

**C** is incorrect because Snowpipe Streaming is ingestion-related.

**D** is incorrect because Marketplace is data/product sharing.

[Search optimization service](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/search-optimization-service)

questions.stem: Which statements about streams are true? (Select TWO)

options.text: Streams are often paired with tasks for incremental processing
Streams are the same as warehouses

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because streams capture changes and are frequently paired with tasks to automate incremental ELT pipelines.

**C** is incorrect because themes belong to UI tools, not streams.

**D** is incorrect because warehouses provide compute, not change tracking.

[Streams introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro)

questions.stem: A dynamic table chain is not meeting freshness expectations. Which property should the engineer review first?

options.text: The password policy on the account
The network MTU of the office router

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because freshness issues on dynamic tables are most directly understood by reviewing target lag expectations and actual refresh history.

**B** is incorrect because password policy is unrelated.

**C** is incorrect because theme settings are irrelevant.

**D** is incorrect because office router MTU is not the first Snowflake check.

[Monitor dynamic tables](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables-monitor)

questions.stem: Which function is commonly used to explode arrays or objects from semi-structured data into rows?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `FLATTEN` turns nested semi-structured elements into a relational result set for transformation.

**B** is incorrect because that converts to geography types.

**C** is incorrect because that is unrelated to semi-structured expansion.

**D** is incorrect because `SHOW PARAMETERS` inspects settings.

[FLATTEN table function](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/flatten)

questions.stem: Which objects are directly relevant to Snowflake file ingestion? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because stages hold or reference files, and Snowpipe automates loading them.

**C** is incorrect because warehouse tags are metadata, not ingestion plumbing.

**D** is incorrect because row access policies govern reads, not file loading.

[Data loading overview](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-overview)

questions.stem: A developer needs reusable procedural logic that can execute inside Snowflake and call Snowpark code. Which object should they create?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because stored procedures are the Snowflake object for procedural logic and can be implemented in supported languages using Snowpark.

**B** is incorrect because masking policies redact data values.

**C** is incorrect because file formats describe staged file structure.

**D** is incorrect because network rules do not provide procedural logic.

[Snowpark stored procedures](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/stored-procedure/stored-procedures-overview)

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of clustering keys in Snowflake?

options.text: To improve pruning for certain query patterns over large tables
To create indexes identical to OLTP systems

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because clustering keys help maintain micro-partition organization so pruning can work better for targeted query predicates.

**B** is incorrect because Snowflake does not use traditional user-managed indexes here.

**C** is incorrect because tasks are an orchestration feature.

**D** is incorrect because role inheritance is a security concept.

[Clustering keys](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys)

questions.stem: Why would a team suspend a task?

options.text: To temporarily stop scheduled or dependent execution
To convert a standard table to an external table

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because suspending a task pauses execution until it is resumed.

**B** is incorrect because compression is automatic storage behavior.

**C** is incorrect because Time Travel is independent.

**D** is incorrect because table type conversion is unrelated.

[Tasks introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-intro)

questions.stem: Which statements about dynamic tables are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: They are refreshed from a defining query
They are used for declarative pipeline design
They replace every stream use case
They are the same object type as stages

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because dynamic tables materialize query results and fit declarative pipeline patterns.

**C** is incorrect because streams still remain useful for other CDC-driven workflows.

**D** is incorrect because stages are separate object types.

[Dynamic tables introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables-intro)

questions.stem: A pipeline must update existing dimension rows and insert new ones in one statement. Which command is best suited?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `MERGE` is designed for synchronized insert/update logic in one transactional statement.

**B** is incorrect because `TRUNCATE` removes all rows.

**C** is incorrect because describing a warehouse only inspects metadata.

**D** is incorrect because altering a user is administrative.

[MERGE command](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/merge)

questions.stem: Why might a data engineer choose Snowpark over only writing raw SQL for part of a pipeline? (Select TWO)

options.text: To use DataFrame APIs in a programming language
To package procedural transformation logic more naturally
To avoid all Snowflake security controls
To eliminate all query optimization concerns

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpark is valuable when engineers want language-native abstractions and richer procedural logic while still processing in Snowflake.

**C** is incorrect because security controls still apply.

**D** is incorrect because performance design still matters.

[Snowpark developer guide](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index)

questions.stem: A team wants to land partner files in an external location and define how CSV parsing should work during load. Which pair of Snowflake concepts is most relevant?

options.text: Masking policy and row access policy

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because stages point to file locations and file formats describe how Snowflake should parse those files during load.

**B** is incorrect because those control data access, not CSV parsing.

**C** is incorrect because those concern performance and recovery.

**D** is incorrect because sharing/listings distribute data products.

[File formats for loading data](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-create-stage)

questions.stem: Which actions are aligned with Snowflake performance investigation? (Select TWO)

options.text: Assume bigger warehouses always solve every issue

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because query profiles and pruning behavior are central to diagnosing Snowflake query performance.

**C** is incorrect because larger warehouses can help some cases but are not a universal fix.

**D** is incorrect because filter selectivity strongly affects performance.

[Query profile](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-query-profile)

questions.stem: A stream appears stale because upstream changes are no longer retained long enough. What broad issue should the engineer investigate?

options.text: Whether change tracking and consumption timing align with retention windows
Whether the Snowsight dark theme is enabled
Whether a warehouse has a comment
Whether the current role owns a sequence

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because staleness is fundamentally about whether the stream can still reference the needed change history before retention windows expire.

**B** is incorrect because theme settings are irrelevant.

**C** is incorrect because warehouse comments do not affect stream freshness.

**D** is incorrect because sequence ownership is unrelated.

[Streams introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro)

questions.stem: Which dynamic table concept is most directly about freshness expectations?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `TARGET_LAG` specifies the desired lag between a dynamic table and its source data.

**B** is incorrect because warehouse size is compute configuration.

**C** is incorrect because comments are metadata.

**D** is incorrect because tag values are governance metadata.

[Dynamic tables target lag](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables-target-lag)

questions.stem: Which interface is most associated with Snowpark transformations?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpark commonly uses DataFrame APIs in supported programming languages.

**B** is incorrect because DNS records are networking objects.

**C** is incorrect because S3 buckets are storage objects outside Snowflake.

**D** is incorrect because BGP sessions are networking concepts.

[Snowpark developer guide](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index)

questions.stem: Which are examples of transformation tasks often handled in Snowflake SQL? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because advanced Snowflake SQL often includes analytic filtering and semi-structured transformations.

**C** is incorrect because SSO IdP configuration is identity administration.

**D** is incorrect because VPC peering is network setup.

[QUALIFY construct](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/constructs/qualify)

questions.stem: A near-real-time ingestion design must minimize manual polling of cloud storage. Which Snowflake option best supports that goal?

options.text: Time Travel queries on the target table

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpipe auto-ingest integrates with cloud event notifications so file discovery and loading do not depend on frequent manual polling.

**B** is incorrect because hourly manual copy is not near-real-time and increases ops burden.

**C** is incorrect because Time Travel does not ingest files.

**D** is incorrect because search optimization is for query performance.

[Snowpipe auto-ingest](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-snowpipe-auto)

questions.stem: A filter on a high-cardinality column still scans too much data because the table is poorly organized. Which feature may help over time?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because clustering can improve the organization of micro-partitions so pruning becomes more effective for targeted predicates.

**B** is incorrect because masking policies affect visibility, not layout.

**C** is incorrect because shares distribute data.

**D** is incorrect because task predecessors affect execution order.

[Clustering keys](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-clustering-keys)

questions.stem: Which task-graph statements are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: A task can depend on another task
Task graphs help orchestrate multi-step ELT pipelines
Tasks are only for identity management
Task graphs remove the need for SQL transformations

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because task graphs coordinate multi-step processing by expressing dependencies between tasks.

**C** is incorrect because tasks are not identity tools.

**D** is incorrect because they orchestrate work but do not remove transformation logic.

[Task graphs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tasks-graphs)

questions.stem: A team wants to keep raw data files in cloud storage and define reusable parsing rules for JSON ingestion. Which Snowflake object complements the stage by describing file structure?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because file formats specify how Snowflake should parse staged data such as CSV, JSON, and Parquet during load operations.

**B** is incorrect because resource monitors track credit consumption.

**C** is incorrect because replication groups handle continuity.

**D** is incorrect because tags are governance metadata.

[File formats for data loading](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-create-stage)

questions.stem: Which pairing is most common for incremental ELT in Snowflake when new row changes should trigger downstream SQL processing?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because streams capture changes and tasks schedule or orchestrate the downstream SQL that processes those changes.

**B** is incorrect because tags and comments are metadata only.

**C** is incorrect because shares and listings distribute data products.

**D** is incorrect because warehouses and roles are necessary platform objects but not the CDC orchestration pattern.

[Streams introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/streams-intro)

questions.stem: Why might an engineer choose a dynamic table? (Select TWO)

options.text: To maintain a transformed result from a query automatically
To replace every security policy object
To serve as an external file stage

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because dynamic tables are for automatically maintained query results with declarative freshness objectives.

**C** is incorrect because security policy objects still remain separate.

**D** is incorrect because stages reference files; they are different objects.

[Dynamic tables introduction](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/dynamic-tables-intro)

questions.stem: A Python engineer wants to package business logic that runs within Snowflake while keeping the computation close to the data. Which Snowflake capability is the strongest fit?

options.text: Snowpark with a stored procedure or UDF

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Snowpark combined with in-platform programmable objects such as stored procedures or UDFs lets developers keep business logic close to Snowflake data.

**B** is incorrect because browser SSO concerns authentication.

**C** is incorrect because Marketplace listings distribute products.

**D** is incorrect because masking handles data protection but not general business logic execution.

[Snowpark developer guide](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark/index)
